mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a rank IC greater than 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening cycles yield negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day forward returns, yielding a rank IC below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 207.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary policy, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 207.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth signal predicts negative 60-day returns in >50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signal an imminent supply-side contraction and earnings recession, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases.
